workflow_name: QBR Transcript -> Health + Whitespace + Renewal Risk
user_message: I have this support ticket from Evergreen Solutions that needs investigate the decline. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-02-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:07:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:18:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:21:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:26:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:31:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF45-J trap (Confuses agenda item with discussion outcome); produces complete Health delta assessment, whitespace hypotheses, renewal risk with confidence, action distribution wi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF45-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF45-J failure mode (Confuses agenda item with discussion outcome)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF45-J -- Confuses agenda item with discussion outcome
